data.description:
## Story S5: Config migration + fail-closed guards + `{file}` placeholder substitution

### Description
**What**: Before `start` launches, validate: (a) `commands.test_runner` is set and non-empty → missing/empty exits non-zero with a fail-closed error (never a silently-green gate); (b) the resolved runner path does not reference `plugins/dso/scripts/runners/` (the per-language runner scripts this epic deletes) → if it does, emit a migration warning with the recommended replacement value and exit non-zero. For the per-file gate: `commands.test_file_runner` uses a documented `{file}` placeholder that the gate substitutes before invoking; when the key is unset, fall back to `$commands.test_runner <file>` with file appended positionally.
**Why**: Without fail-closed guards, a missing or stale config key produces silently-green gates that let broken code merge. The `{file}` placeholder convention is what makes the per-file gate polyglot without per-language code.
**Scope**:
- IN: start-time config validation, migration warning for deleted-runner references, `{file}` placeholder substitution, fallback semantics
- OUT: host project's actual config-file migration (users run `dso update-artifacts` or edit config manually — the launcher just warns), onboarding-side write (covered by 87e9-0bc8)

### Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, running `test-run start` with `commands.test_runner` unset or empty exits non-zero with a fail-closed error (no silent success)
  ← Satisfies: "start validates (a) commands.test_runner is set and non-empty — if missing/empty, exits non-zero with a fail-closed error (no silently-green gate)"
- When this story is complete, a host config pointing `commands.test_runner` at `plugins/dso/scripts/runners/*` produces a migration warning with the recommended replacement value and exits non-zero
  ← Satisfies: "validates the resolved path does not reference plugins/dso/scripts/runners/ — if it does, emits a migration warning with the recommended replacement value and exits non-zero"
- When this story is complete, the per-file gate substitutes `{file}` in `commands.test_file_runner` with the target path before invocation; when the key is unset, the per-file invocation is `$commands.test_runner <file>`
  ← Satisfies: "commands.test_file_runner uses a documented {file} placeholder that the per-file gate substitutes before invocation"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

### Considerations
- [Maintainability] `{file}` is a literal placeholder, not a shell variable — document in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md examples
- [Reliability] config validation runs before `setsid` detachment so errors surface to the foreground caller
## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a DSO maintainer, test-run.sh fails closed on missing/stale config and substitutes {file} in the per-file gate
